American guitarist and composer

Musical artist

**John Doan** (born May 16, 1951) is an American guitarist and composer.[[1]](#cite_note-1)

## Background

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=John_Doan&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Background")]

Doan grew up in [Venice, California](/wiki/Venice,_California "Venice, California"), and at the age of eleven began playing the guitar, first a 12-string, and later a double-neck electric in a rock band. Later, while studying music at [California State University, Northridge](/wiki/California_State_University,_Northridge "California State University, Northridge") he was introduced to classical guitar. He really enjoyed the music for the lute and was amazed at the sound of its many strings. Later when he found a century-old harp-guitar on the back wall of a music store, it called to him with its beautiful shape and unusual collection of extra strings. He relates: "I was achingly curious and wanted to transform its silence and neglect into something alive and vibrant. It was and continues to be an adventure to play music on the harp-guitar."[[2]](#cite_note-2)
After moving to Oregon, Doan earned his master's in musical education from [Western Oregon University](/wiki/Western_Oregon_University "Western Oregon University") and served on the faculty there. He studied the renaissance and baroque lute in the Netherlands.[[3]](#cite_note-3) Doan's music has a strong classical influence and he also finds inspiration in folk traditions, Irish musical traditions in particular. He is a Professor Emeritus of Music at [Willamette University](/wiki/Willamette_University "Willamette University") in [Salem, Oregon](/wiki/Salem,_Oregon "Salem, Oregon"), a historian, and a multi-instrumentalist specializing in unusual vintage instruments.[[4]](#cite_note-4) Over the years Doan has played with many folk and country artists, including [Donovan](/wiki/Donovan "Donovan"), [Burl Ives](/wiki/Burl_Ives "Burl Ives"), [Larry Carlton](/wiki/Larry_Carlton "Larry Carlton"), [Chet Atkins](/wiki/Chet_Atkins "Chet Atkins") and [Mason Williams](/wiki/Mason_Williams "Mason Williams").[[5]](#cite_note-Bio:_All_Music-5)

## Recordings

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=John_Doan&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Recordings")]

Doan created his own brand of folk-fusion on his first album on [Narada](/wiki/Narada_Productions "Narada Productions"), titled *Departures* (1988).[[5]](#cite_note-Bio:_All_Music-5) He followed that up with *Remembrance: Melodies from a Forgotten Era* (1993) on the [Tapestry](/wiki/Capri_Records_(Jazz_record_label) "Capri Records (Jazz record label)") label which was inspired by his own musical traditions in folk tunes of the American West.[[6]](#cite_note-6) In the 1990s two television specials were produced by [Oregon Public Broadcasting](/wiki/Oregon_Public_Broadcasting "Oregon Public Broadcasting"): *A Christmas to Remember With John Doan* (seen on [PBS](/wiki/PBS "PBS")) and his Emmy nominated *A Victorian Christmas With John Doan*. The live version of his *Victorian Christmas,* in which he re-enacts what it might have been like to celebrate Christmas a century ago, has been a holiday tradition for over 30 years.[[7]](#cite_note-7) His recording on [Hearts of Space](/wiki/Hearts_of_Space_Records "Hearts of Space Records"), *Eire – Isle of the Saints,* won "Best Celtic Album of the Year." *The Lost Music of Fernando Sor* (2008) features the music of [Fernando Sor](/wiki/Fernando_Sor "Fernando Sor") (1778–1839), beloved as the "Father of the Classical Guitar", who composed ten works for the [harpolyre](/w/index.php?title=Harpolyre&action=edit&redlink=1 "Harpolyre (page does not exist)"), a three-necked, 21 string guitar. Doan discovered and restored a playable instrument from c. 1830 which he used for this recording.[[8]](#cite_note-8) Doan's latest video project is a 90-minute documentary that he wrote and starred in titled *In Search of the Harp Guitar.* John hosted an International Harp Guitar Festival at Willamette University in Salem, Oregon.[[9]](#cite_note-9) Doan was recently awarded "World's Leading Harp Guitarist" by the Brand Laureate Awards.

## Discography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=John_Doan&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Discography")]

* *Into the Quiet* (2016)
* *Homage to Fernando Sor* (2012)
* *Harp Guitars Under the Stars* (2010) (with [Muriel Anderson](/wiki/Muriel_Anderson "Muriel Anderson"))
* *A Celtic Pilgrimage* (2010)
* *The Lost Music of Fernando Sor* (2008)
* *Wayfarer: Ancient Paths to Sacred Places* (1999)
* *Eire: Isle of the Saints (A Celtic Odyssey)* (1997)
* *Wrapped in White: Visions of Christmas Past* (1994)
* *Remembrance: Melodies from a Forgotten Era* (1993)
* *Departures* (1988)

**Compilations**

* *Brad Hoyt | Together Alone| Duets featuring Piano and Harp Guitar* (2009)
* *Harp Guitar Dreams* (2008)
* *Christmas Past: Holiday Harp Guitar Classics* (2006)
* *Beyond Six Strings* (2006)
* Windham Hill: The Renaissance Album (1998)
* *Renaissance: A Midsummer Night's Dream*
* *Celtic Twilight I–V*

* *In Search of the Harp Guitar* (2006)
